items.how_used: 论文在第二章列出了7条假设，包括无人机视为质点、忽略风力和空气阻力、所有无人机性能相同、电池足够等，为后续建模简化条件。
items.source_excerpt: 2.1 Assumptions: 1. The drone is considered as a particle. 2. The wind and air resistance are ignored. 3. All drones have the same performance. 4. The battery can support the whole performance. ...

items.how_used: 论文定义了变量表，包括无人机数量N、固定点坐标(x0,y0,z0)、每个无人机的位置(xi,yi,zi)、速度v、时间t等，用于后续公式推导。
items.source_excerpt: 2.2 Variables: N: number of drones; (x0,y0,z0): fixed point coordinates; (xi,yi,zi): position of drone i; v: speed of drone; t: time.

items.how_used: 对于摩天轮图案，使用圆的参数方程描述每个无人机的位置，并加入旋转角度随时间线性变化实现旋转。
items.source_excerpt: For the Ferris wheel, the position of each drone is given by: x_i(t)=x0+R cos(θ0+ωt+2πi/N), y_i(t)=y0+R sin(θ0+ωt+2πi/N).

items.how_used: 在图案切换时，论文设计了密集散射扩散算法（Dense Scatter Diffusion Algorithm），将无人机从当前图案位置移动到下一图案位置，通过求解最短路径（类似TSP）来规划轨迹，避免碰撞。
items.source_excerpt: We creatively designed the dense scatter diffusion algorithm to find the shortest possible time to calculate the path and avoid collision, the fast anti-collision switch of the patterns in the air can be achieved.

items.how_used: 论文通过改变无人机数量、飞行速度等参数，观察表演效果和碰撞风险的变化，验证模型的鲁棒性。
items.source_excerpt: We performed sensitivity analysis by varying the number of drones and speed to check the robustness of the model.

items.how_used: 论文使用了多张示意图和仿真截图展示无人机编队图案（摩天轮、龙、时钟），并配有图标题和编号，增强可读性。
items.source_excerpt: Fig1: Design of taking off and formation of Ferris Wheel; Fig2: Design of Rotation of the Ferris Wheel; Fig3: Design of Dance of the Dragon; Fig4: Design of the Count-Down Clock.

items.how_used: 论文使用MATLAB进行仿真，模拟无人机编队飞行和图案切换，验证模型可行性，并生成可视化结果。
items.source_excerpt: We made a high degree of simulation in Matlab to accurately calculate the number of UAVs required for the performance, the area of the air performance, the UAV launching path, ...

items.how_used: 论文采用IMRaD结构：引言（I）、问题重述（I）、模型建立（M）、结果（R）、讨论（D），并包含摘要、关键词、参考文献、附录等完整部分。
items.source_excerpt: Contents: Summary, I. Introduction and Problem Restatement, II. Definitions, Justifications, Assumptions and Variables, III. Image preprocessing, IV. Mathematical Models, V. Sky light display project design and Simulation effect display, VI. Strengths and Weaknesses, VII. Further Work, VIII. Reference, Appendix.
